message:
feat(pi_legacy_capture): Add --view-log trace viewer for pi.ext.log.v1 JSONL files

Extend pi_legacy_capture binary with a trace viewing mode for analyzing
extension log files produced during capture runs:

CLI interface:
- --view-log PATH: View a pi.ext.log.v1 JSONL file (use "-" for stdin)
- --view-mode {pretty,jsonl}: Output format (default: pretty)
- --view-min-level {debug,info,warn,error}: Minimum log level filter
- --view-extension-id EXT: Filter by extension ID (repeatable)
- --view-scenario-id SCN: Filter by scenario ID (repeatable)
- --view-event-prefix PFX: Filter by event prefix (repeatable)
- --view-no-summary: Suppress the statistics footer

Implementation:
- TraceViewMode enum for output format selection
- TraceViewLevel enum with allows() method for level filtering
- run_trace_viewer() reads JSONL, deserializes LogPayload, applies filters
- Pretty mode renders human-readable lines with timestamp, level, event,
  extension, scenario, and message
- JSONL mode passes through matching records for pipeline processing
- Statistics tracking: total/kept counts, breakdowns by level, event,
  extension, and scenario

Use cases:
- Debug extension execution: quickly scan for errors or specific events
- Pipeline integration: filter and transform logs programmatically
- Audit capability usage: grep for specific extension/scenario combinations

This complements the capture functionality by enabling post-hoc analysis
of extension traces without external tooling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
